message:
fix(fuzz): use checked arithmetic for paren depth tracking

The transpiled-query paren-balance check used a signed counter with
saturating_sub on closing parens:

    let mut depth: i32 = 0;
    ...
    ')' => {
        depth = depth.saturating_sub(1);
        assert!(depth >= 0, "unbalanced closing paren ...");
    }

saturating_sub floors at 0, so a stray `)` with no matching `(` left
depth at 0 — and the `assert!(depth >= 0)` (literally `0 >= 0`) was
always true. The "early closing paren" branch the comment described
was never actually checked, only the "unmatched opener at end"
branch. Any transpiler bug that emitted `)` before `(` would slip
past the fuzz oracle silently.

Switch to a u32 depth counter with checked arithmetic:
- '(' uses checked_add(1).expect(...) — overflows on a pathological
  runaway-`(` input panic with a descriptive message instead of
  silently saturating to u32::MAX and skewing later math.
- ')' uses checked_sub(1).unwrap_or_else(|| panic!(...)) — a stray
  early `)` now trips the assertion immediately, restoring the
  invariant the comment claimed.

The trailing assert_eq!(depth, 0, ...) message is reworded to read
"{depth} unclosed `(` at end" since u32 depth can no longer be
negative.

No production code change — the fuzz target is the libFuzzer harness
only.
